key_highlights: Major geopolitical shock: Trump evacuated after shooting at White House Correspondents' Dinner, gunman detained, security heightened for King Charles' visit.
Hormuz crisis deepens: IEA warns of biggest energy security threat in history, shipping traffic halted, oil traders bet $430M on price drop before ceasefire extension.
Big Tech earnings week make-or-break for rally: Apple, Amazon, Google lead wave; Dow Jones futures fall after Iran talks shelved.
Record-setting momentum rally draws doubters; investors protecting stocks at record levels eye bets on higher rates.
Wall Street split on quantum computing: Goldman and JPMorgan diverge on strategy.
Sen. Tillis expected to clear way for Warsh as Fed Chair; Fed set to lead uneasy G-7 with rates on hold.
Frontier markets rally attracts investors back in after April surge.
Crypto and meme coins gain traction at Mar-a-Lago; institutional Bitcoin adoption rises.
Private credit funds attract bargain hunters; cheap debt draws interest.
Big Tech job cuts seen as economic warning; consumer-facing companies face cost pressures.

sectors.trend: Crisis escalation in Hormuz Strait: IEA calls it biggest energy security threat in history; shipping traffic halted, blockades firm. Oil traders bet $430M on price drop before Trump's ceasefire extension. Lockheed Martin CEO sends strong 2-word message on Middle East.
sectors.implications: Oil prices likely to spike, supply chain disruptions for global trade, defense stocks benefit (Lockheed Martin). Energy security concerns may accelerate renewable and rare earth investments.

sectors.trend: Earnings week make-or-break for rally: Apple, Amazon, Google lead. Quantum computing race splits Wall Street (Goldman vs JPMorgan). Big Tech job cuts seen as economic warning. Tesla's latest update changes bull/bear case. Musk vies to turn X into super app with banking tool.
sectors.implications: High volatility expected; earnings results will set near-term direction. Quantum computing investments may create long-term winners. Job cuts signal cost discipline but also demand weakness. Tesla's update could reshape EV competition.

sectors.trend: Wall Street split on quantum computing (Goldman, JPMorgan). Fed chair transition likely (Warsh). Fed set to lead G-7 with rates on hold. Bond traders await Powell update and Treasury auctions. Private credit funds attract bargain hunters. Credit card fees set new precedent (Amex, Chase).
sectors.implications: Rate-sensitive sectors may benefit from stable rates. Private credit offers yield opportunities but with liquidity risk. Fed leadership change could shift regulatory stance. Consumer finance fees may face regulatory scrutiny.

sectors.trend: Institutional Bitcoin adoption rising; meme coins and crypto at Mar-a-Lago. Polymarket tries to show compliance. Coinbase, SoFi, and other crypto-related stocks under review. Musk's X plans banking tool.
sectors.implications: Crypto market gaining mainstream acceptance but regulatory risks remain. Institutional inflows support prices. Meme coin frenzy may indicate speculative excess. X's banking move could disrupt fintech.

sectors.trend: Investors dive back into frontier markets after April rally. HK IPOs raised $17.9B in 2026. Malaysia protests against anti-graft chief. Hungary's next premier warns against Orban-tied assets.
sectors.implications: Frontier markets offer high growth but high risk. HK IPO market robust, signaling capital flows. Political instability in Malaysia and Hungary may deter investment.

sectors.trend: Lockheed Martin CEO sends strong message on Middle East. GE Aerospace under review. Defense spending likely to increase amid geopolitical tensions.
sectors.implications: Defense stocks may outperform as geopolitical risks rise. GE Aerospace benefits from commercial and military demand.

sectors.trend: Energy Fuels, MP Materials, Lithium Americas, Lithium Argentina highlighted as rare earth plays. BMO Capital touts Lithium Argentina's cost advantage.
sectors.implications: Supply chain diversification and green energy transition drive demand. Rare earth stocks may benefit from policy support and EV adoption.

key_themes: Geopolitical risk: Hormuz crisis, Trump shooting, Iran talks shelved, Russia-North Korea defense visit.
Monetary policy transition: Fed chair change, G-7 rate decisions, bond market positioning.
Earnings season: Big Tech results critical for market direction, consumer and industrial earnings mixed.
Energy security: IEA warning, shipping crisis, oil price volatility, defense spending.
Tech innovation: Quantum computing race, AI venture funding, Tesla updates, X super app.
Crypto adoption: Institutional Bitcoin, meme coins, regulatory compliance.
Frontier markets: Rally attracts capital, but political risks remain.
Consumer pressures: JetBlue data privacy lawsuit, Trader Joe's controversy, dating costs, credit card fees.
Economic warnings: Big Tech job cuts, momentum rally doubts, record hedging against rate hikes.

risk_assessment.risk_factor: Geopolitical escalation: Hormuz blockade and Iran tensions
risk_assessment.mitigation: Increase exposure to energy, defense, and rare earth stocks; hedge with oil futures or energy ETFs; reduce exposure to shipping and import-dependent sectors.

risk_assessment.risk_factor: Political violence and instability: Trump shooting, security threats
risk_assessment.mitigation: Diversify into safe-haven assets (gold, Treasuries); monitor volatility indices; avoid overconcentration in event-sensitive sectors.

risk_assessment.risk_factor: Monetary policy uncertainty: Fed chair transition, rate path
risk_assessment.mitigation: Maintain duration-neutral bond positioning; favor floating-rate instruments; watch Powell updates and Treasury auction results.
Implement stop-losses on high-momentum stocks; rotate to value and defensive sectors; reduce leverage.
Hedge with put options on QQQ or individual tech names; diversify into non-tech sectors; reduce position sizes ahead of earnings.
Allocate to energy producers, renewable energy, and commodities; consider TIPS for inflation protection; reduce exposure to energy-intensive industries.

risk_assessment.risk_factor: Consumer spending slowdown and credit risk
risk_assessment.mitigation: Focus on essential goods and services; avoid overleveraged consumer companies; monitor credit card delinquency data.

risk_assessment.risk_factor: Regulatory and legal risks in crypto and fintech
risk_assessment.mitigation: Limit exposure to unregulated crypto assets; favor compliant platforms like Coinbase; monitor SEC and DOJ actions.

opportunities.recommendation: Increase allocation to energy and defense stocks
opportunities.rationale: Hormuz crisis and Middle East tensions drive demand for oil, defense equipment, and energy security solutions. Lockheed Martin and energy producers likely to benefit.

opportunities.recommendation: Buy rare earth and critical mineral stocks
opportunities.rationale: Supply chain diversification and green energy transition support long-term demand. MP Materials and Lithium Americas are well-positioned.
April rally and strong HK IPO market indicate capital inflows. Focus on countries with stable political environments and growth catalysts.
Big Tech earnings could be volatile but long-term fundamentals remain strong. Quantum computing and AI are secular growth themes.

opportunities.recommendation: Consider private credit funds for yield
opportunities.rationale: Cheap private credit funds offer attractive yields in a stable rate environment. Bargain hunters are entering the space.
tickers: Private credit ETFs like PCSVX, BBDCX

defensive_moves.recommendation: Hedge against geopolitical risk with gold and Treasuries
defensive_moves.rationale: Safe-haven assets provide protection against political violence and energy crisis. Gold and long-duration Treasuries are traditional hedges.
Record rally drawing doubters; momentum could reverse sharply. Implement trailing stops or take partial profits.
tickers: Avoid overvalued momentum names; consider VTV, IWD for value rotation

defensive_moves.rationale: Uncertainty around Fed chair transition, earnings, and geopolitics warrants higher liquidity. Cash provides optionality to deploy during dips.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Buy put options on oil or energy ETFs
defensive_moves.rationale: Traders bet $430M on oil price drop; hedging against a potential ceasefire or demand shock. Protect long energy positions.
Consumer spending slowdown and economic warnings favor defensive sectors. Molina Healthcare and utilities offer stable earnings.

market_outlook.short_term: 1-3 month outlook: Highly volatile due to geopolitical shocks (Hormuz crisis, Trump shooting), Big Tech earnings, and Fed chair transition. Energy and defense likely to outperform. Momentum rally at risk of correction. Safe-haven assets (gold, Treasuries) will be in demand. Oil prices may spike further if Hormuz blockade persists.
market_outlook.long_term: 6-12 month outlook: Secular trends in quantum computing, AI, rare earths, and energy security will drive long-term growth. Fed policy normalization and geopolitical stability could restore risk appetite. Frontier markets and private credit offer alpha. Inflation and supply chain disruptions remain key risks.
key_catalysts: Big Tech earnings results (Apple, Amazon, Google) this week
Fed chair nomination and Senate confirmation (Warsh)
Hormuz ceasefire negotiations and oil price trajectory
Trump shooting investigation and political stability
G-7 rate decisions and Powell's commentary
US Treasury auctions and bond market reaction
King Charles' state visit security implications
HK IPO pipeline and frontier market inflows
Crypto regulatory clarity and institutional adoption
watch_list: Oil prices (WTI, Brent) and shipping traffic in Hormuz
Big Tech earnings reports and forward guidance
Fed chair transition updates and Senate hearings
Crypto market cap and Bitcoin ETF flows
Geopolitical developments in Middle East, Ukraine, and Asia
Private credit fund performance and inflows
